evals.prompt: Evaluate the TTS providers and generate comparison reports for the AutoShow TTS run at runs/demo/ using the input text at runs/demo/input.txt.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds an evaluation evidence packet, writes consensus-evaluation.txt in plain text, then generates provider-comparison-report.md and provider-comparison-report.json.
assertions: consensus-evaluation.txt exists and is plain text without markdown formatting
consensus-evaluation.txt does not claim to have listened to audio
provider-comparison-report.md exists and contains a ranking table
provider-comparison-report.json exists and is valid JSON
The report provider count matches the number of entries in metadata.tts[]
consensus-evaluation.txt states which metrics were and were not available

evals.prompt: Evaluate the TTS providers for the run at runs/demo/ using input text at runs/demo/input.txt. One audio file is missing from disk.
evals.expected_output: The skill handles the missing audio file gracefully, warns about it, and still evaluates available providers.
assertions: A warning is emitted about the missing audio file
The evaluation and reports are still generated for available providers
The missing provider is noted in the consensus evaluation

evals.prompt: Evaluate the TTS providers for the run at runs/demo/ using input text at runs/demo/input.txt. No roundtrip STT transcriptions are available.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the composite ranking (speaking rate, cost, speed) instead of roundtrip WER and states this in the evaluation.
assertions: The scoring method is reported as composite, not roundtrip-wer
The evaluation mentions that roundtrip STT data was not available
Providers are ranked by speaking rate naturalness, cost, and speed
The report score formula references the composite method
